logs.title: MBR and EBR ingested — MTX-2024-0204 loaded for review
reasoning: MBR: MPR-MTX-2.5-v05, Methotrexate Tablets 2.5mg, batch size 200,000 tablets, 40.000 kg
EBR: Batch MTX-2024-0204, manufactured 12–15 Feb 2024, Building B OSD Cytotoxic Suite
MBR extracted: formula, equipment list, 72-point checklist, hold times, EM specs, QC specs
EBR extracted: all executed steps, actual values, QC results, mass balance, disposition
Both documents fully loaded — proceeding to field-by-field comparison

logs.title: All process parameters and QC results pass — assay 99.4%, dissolution min 97.1%
reasoning: Dispensing: API MTX USP 500.015g dispensed (spec 499.5–500.5g) PASS; triple independent verification (M. Chen, D. Okonkwo, P. Sharma) all different individuals, all signed PASS
Blending: pre-lubrication 15 RPM × 20 min, BU mean 100.02%, RSD 4.8% (≤6.0%), HP-01 hold time 0.75hr vs 72hr limit — all PASS
Compression: all 17 CPP monitoring entries in spec, all 9 IPC time points pass, compression yield 99.88% (≥97.0%) PASS; all inter-step hold times within validated limits
QC: assay 99.4% (spec 90–110%), CU AV=3.8 (≤15.0), dissolution min 97.1% (Q≥80%), related substances 0.3% total (≦2.0%) — all PASS
EM compliant all rooms and days; IH wipe sampling at all 10 locations below OEL-derived limit — no containment breach

logs.title: MAJOR: API mass balance transcription error — 5,000g recorded vs 500.015g actual — Confidence: 92%
reasoning: EBR Section 8.1 records API dispensed (Item A) as 5,000.0g — this is a 10× transcription error; dispensing log (Section 2.2) clearly records 500.015g dispensed
Consequence: calculated accountability = 515.695 / 5,000.0 = 9.8% with 4,484g listed as unaccounted — both figures are nonsensical
EBR checklist Item 52 marked Y (pass) by S. Williams for mass balance accuracy — incorrect; error not detected during QA review
Finding type: api_mass_balance_transcription_error — MAJOR documentation deficiency under 21 CFR 211.192
This is a documentation/transcription error, not a product quality failure — actual API dispensed and in-process data are internally consistent; confidence 92% — HOLD pending correction

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: Mass balance Section 8.1 Item A: 5,000.0g recorded — actual dispensed per Section 2.2 is 500.015g (10× transcription error)
Accountability calculation (9.8%, 4,484g unaccounted) is nonsensical — GMP-critical documentation deficiency under 21 CFR 211.192
QA reviewer S. Williams signed off checklist Item 52 as pass without detecting the error
All QC results (assay 99.4%, CU AV 3.8, dissolution 97.1%) confirm product quality is not in question
SOP-DOC-001 correction by K. Yamamoto and re-sign by S. Williams required; no product quality investigation needed

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — API Mass Balance Transcription Error
artifacts.subject: HOLD: Mass Balance Transcription Error — Batch MTX-2024-0204 Methotrexate 2.5mg
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch MTX-2024-0204 (Methotrexate Tablets 2.5mg) requires a documentation correction before release.

MAJOR FINDING — API Mass Balance Transcription Error:
EBR Section 8.1 Item A (API dispensed quantity) records 5,000.0 g. The dispensing log (Section 2.2) records the actual dispensed quantity as 500.015 g. This is a 10x transcription error.

Impact: The accountability calculation (9.8%) and unaccounted API (4,484 g) are nonsensical. GMP-critical documentation deficiency under 21 CFR 211.192. QA reviewer S. Williams signed off checklist Item 52 as pass without detecting the error.

Note: All QC results are within specification (assay 99.4%, CU AV 3.8, dissolution min 97.1%). This is a documentation issue, not a product quality failure.

Required actions:
1. Return EBR to Production Manager K. Yamamoto for SOP-DOC-001 correction (correct value: 500.015 g in Section 8.1 Item A)
2. QA Reviewer S. Williams to re-sign checklist Item 52 after correction is verified
3. No product quality investigation required

Batch remains on HOLD until correction is completed.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
